mission.title: Install and Configure Kairos on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kairos is an immutable Linux meta-distribution designed for edge Kubernetes, enabling the creation of bootable Kubernetes and OS images for edge devices. Installing Kairos allows you to manage cluster lifecycle seamlessly and securely, ensuring no infrastructure drift.

steps.title: Download Kairos ISO
steps.description:
Download the Kairos ISO for your target architecture. Kairos v4.0.3 uses the Hadron image format:
```bash
# Standard image with K3s v1.35.2 (recommended)
curl -Lo kairos.iso https://github.com/kairos-io/kairos/releases/download/v4.0.3/kairos-hadron-v0.0.4-standard-amd64-generic-v4.0.3-k3sv1.35.2+k3s1.iso
```
Other K3s versions are available. See all flavors at https://github.com/kairos-io/kairos/releases/tag/v4.0.3

steps.title: Boot and install interactively
steps.description:
Boot the ISO on your edge device (USB, PXE, or VM). From the GRUB menu, select `kairos (interactive install)` to launch the guided installer.

Alternatively, from the LiveCD terminal run:
```bash
kairos-agent interactive-install
```

The installer walks you through network, user, and K3s configuration. The resulting config is saved to `/oem/90_custom.yaml`.

steps.title: Automated install (optional)
steps.description:
For zero-touch provisioning, create a `cloud-config.yaml`:
```yaml
#cloud-config
install:
  device: "/dev/sda"
  reboot: true
  auto: true
users:
  - name: kairos
    passwd: kairos
```

Supply it via one of these methods:
- **ISO datasource**: package it as a cidata ISO attached at boot
- **URL**: add `config_url=https://example.com/cloud-config.yaml` to boot parameters
- **Embedded**: remaster the ISO with AuroraBoot:
```bash
docker run -v $PWD/cloud-config.yaml:/cloud-config.yaml \
  -v $PWD/build:/tmp/auroraboot \
  -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock \
  --rm -ti quay.io/kairos/auroraboot \
  --set container_image=oci:$IMAGE \
  --cloud-config /cloud-config.yaml \
  --set "state_dir=/tmp/auroraboot"
```

steps.title: Verify the Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
After installation and reboot, SSH into the node and verify K3s is running:
```bash
ssh kairos@<node-ip>
sudo k3s kubectl get nodes
sudo k3s kubectl get pods -A
```
All system pods should be in `Running` state.

resolution.summary: Kairos is installed as an immutable Linux OS with K3s for edge computing. Nodes are managed via cloud-config and upgraded atomically via OCI container images with A/B rollback support.

uninstall.title: Reinstall the OS
uninstall.description: Kairos is an immutable OS — to remove it, reinstall a different OS on the device.

upgrade.title: List available versions
upgrade.description:
```bash
sudo kairos-agent upgrade list-releases
```
Use `--registry` to specify a custom registry (defaults to quay.io/kairos).

upgrade.title: Upgrade the active system
upgrade.description:
Upgrade atomically via OCI image:
```bash
sudo kairos-agent upgrade --source oci:quay.io/kairos/hadron:v0.0.4-standard-amd64-generic-v4.0.3-k3sv1.35.2-k3s1
```
Test the new system before upgrading recovery.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the recovery system
upgrade.description:
Only after confirming the active system works:
```bash
sudo kairos-agent upgrade --recovery --source oci:quay.io/kairos/hadron:v0.0.4-standard-amd64-generic-v4.0.3-k3sv1.35.2-k3s1
```

troubleshooting.title: Node fails to boot after install
troubleshooting.description: Check the install logs in `/var/log/kairos/` and ensure the target disk device path is correct in cloud-config.

troubleshooting.title: K3s not starting
troubleshooting.description:
Check the K3s service:
```bash
sudo systemctl status k3s
sudo journalctl -u k3s
```

troubleshooting.title: P2P mesh not forming
troubleshooting.description:
Kairos P2P mesh requires UDP port 51820 (WireGuard) to be open between nodes. Verify with:
```bash
sudo ss -ulnp | grep 51820
```
